channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: With Welcome Center closed in Brownsville, NGO focusing on helping migrants south of border
item.description: A migrant shelter in this border town has closed after a sudden decrease in asylum seekers due to new immigration policies implemented by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Florida leaders warn deadly consequences of ‘senior assassin’ game
item.description: They are urging parents and guardians to discuss with their children the potential dangers of playing this game.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, NewsNation

item.title: Snowy roads lead to hundreds of Virginia and North Carolina crashes as Arctic air brings record cold
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Snowy roads led to hundreds of crashes throughout Virginia and North Carolina, including a series of crashes on an interstate highway involving more than 50 vehicles, as officials continued to ask people Thursday to avoid travel to allow crews to work on the roads.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Senate GOP dismayed by Trump, Zelensky war of words
item.description: Public comments from President Trump blaming Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for Russia’s invasion of his country are unnerving Senate Republicans, who have largely sought to avoid conflicts with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE dividend checks a possibility, Trump says
item.description: (KRON) -- On Wednesday, President Donald Trump signaled he was open to the possibility of sending out stimulus checks to Americans using funds cut from the federal budget by Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. The idea was initially floated on social media by Azoria CEO James Fishback.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Sen. Mitch McConnell won’t seek reelection next year
item.description: Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell is announcing that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decades-long tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Creative control of James Bond franchise given to Amazon MGM
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios and longtime Bond custodians Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli formed a new joint venture in which they will co-own James Bond intellectual property rights — but Amazon MGM will have creative control.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, KRON4

item.title: McConnell won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) will announce Thursday that he is not seeking reelection in 2026, bringing an end to the longest-serving Senate leader’s political career. “Seven times, my fellow Kentuckians have sent me to the Senate,” McConnell will say, according to prepared remarks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EU official meets with Trump counterparts to resolve tariff threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hoping to head off a potential trade conflict, a top European Union official stressed the importance of active engagement and fairness in trade during a four-hour meeting with Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: David Boren, a former Oklahoma governor and veteran US senator and university president, dies at 83
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Former Oklahoma Gov. David Boren, who became one of the nation's youngest governors in the 1970s at age 33 and later helped shape national intelligence as a U.S. senator, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: TIM TALLEY and SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Russian aircraft fly in Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone, US says
item.description: Russian military aircraft were spotted flying in the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone (ADIZ) on Tuesday and Wednesday, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies. State and local health departments are only just beginning to hear from officials at the CDC, nearly a month after the inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: The IRS plans to lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This rookie QB card could be worth $50K if you pull it from a new Topps set
item.description: Denver Broncos rookie quarterback Bo Nix impressed on the field, and now a Colorado sports card store is ready to pay $50,000 to keep a unique card featuring him close to home.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, KRON4

item.title: DOGE notches courtroom wins as Elon Musk crusades to slash federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Although some parts of President Donald Trump ’s agenda are getting bogged down by litigation, Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency is having better luck in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 accused of yelling 'white power', 'Nazi power' at Denver protesters arrested
item.description: The two also threw glass objects at the women and drove away, but not before one displayed a Nazi salute, police said.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Feds pause contract providing legal services to thousands of unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – An El Paso nonprofit plans to sue the federal government for halting their ability to provide legal services to migrant minors in federal custody.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Department of Homeland Security fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: The white man who pleaded guilty to shooting a Black teen who rang a wrong doorbell dies
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — An 86-year-old Missouri man has died just days after pleading guilty to a lesser charge in the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, prosecutors announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds rally against firing of federal workers, emphasizing threats to public health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hundreds of current and former federal employees rallied Wednesday outside the Department of Health and Human Services headquarters, protesting recent firings and their effect on public health, and directing much of their ire at Elon Musk and his agency's efforts to cut spending.
item.dc:creator: NATHAN ELLGREN and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Seven Chilean men are charged with burglarizing the homes of Mahomes, Burrow and other star athletes
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Seven men from Chile have been charged in Florida federal court with orchestrating burglaries at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country, according to a criminal complaint.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: Federal government pulls approval for NYC congestion pricing
item.description: "New York State’s congestion pricing plan is a slap in the face to working class Americans and small business owners,” Secretary of Transportation Sean Duffy said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris signs with major Hollywood talent agency
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris has signed with the Creative Artists Agency, one of Hollywood's top talent and sports agencies. In a post on Instagram, CAA revealed signed with the agency "for representation in all areas, with a focus on speaking engagements and publishing.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Jan. 6 pardon recipients argue it stretches to other crimes: report
item.description: Many defendants are arguing that crimes that were found during the riot investigations should be pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon bill would allow law enforcement to destroy hoop houses at illegal grows
item.description: Senate Bill 162 seeks to give law enforcement the authority to use heavy machinery to destroy the hoop houses — outdoor structures used to farm marijuana plants.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multi-state killings held without bail
item.description: The Zizians have been tied to the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David Maland near the Canadian border in January and five other homicides in Vermont, Pennsylvania and California.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delta jet flips upside down on a snowy Toronto runway and all 80 aboard survive
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A Delta Air Lines jet flipped on its roof while landing Monday at Toronto’s Pearson Airport, but all 80 people on board survived and those hurt had relatively minor injuries, the airport’s chief executive said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4
